claim: On December 10, 2007, former CIA analyst and case officer John Kiriakou became the first U.S. intelligence officer to publicly confirm on television that the CIA had used waterboarding on a high-value al-Qaeda detainee, in an interview with ABC News reporters Brian Ross and Richard Esposito.

sources.label: ABC News, “Coming in From the Cold: CIA Spy Calls Waterboarding Necessary But Torture,” Brian Ross & Richard Esposito (Dec 10, 2007)
sources.quote: The threat information he provided disrupted a number of attacks, maybe dozens of attacks

sources.label: Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: ABC News URL fetched and confirmed during research pass (2026-05-11): article documents 'the first public comment by any CIA officer involved in handling high-value al Qaeda targets' on the agency's use of waterboarding, with verbatim Kiriakou quotes. The reel is cited as the surfacing source; the substantive claim rests on the ABC News primary.
